It is time to reawaken your mind and body so you can resume your regular activities.In a moment I will count to three. When you hear the number three you you are completely awake and alert, filled with energy and confidence.One…Two…Three.Continue from Martial Arts Training Guided Imagery toPreparation for Martial Arts CompetitionBack toRelaxation ScriptsBack toInner Health Studio Home

Clinch and left back knee.Clinch, front knee, back knee.Now imagine practicing any other knees or elbows you want to do…practicing both front and back, both sides right and left, to the head and to the body. Imagine whatever knee or elbow strikes you decide…such as reverse elbow…flying knee…side knee…(Pause)Now that you have rehearsed the basic martial arts training techniques, imagine yourself in a martial arts competition. Imagine facing your opponent. See yourself moving with your opponent…responding at the same time as your opponent moves.See yourself moving and circling…evading your opponent’s strikes…See yourself block kicks and punches, and as you block, executing offensive moves of your own…Imagine blocking effectively as you strike, refusing to leave any opening for your opponent to strike you…Visualize yourself striking with fast, powerful, effective combinations. See each blow landing exactly as intended to…wearing down your opponent…scoring points…each strike moving you one step closer to winning the martial arts competition.See in detail the combinations. Imagine confronting your opponent with combinations. Breathe with each strike. Block effectively with each strike. Keep circling. Eyes open when you block and open when you strike. Deliberately timing your blinking. Fully in control. Maintain good fighting stance. Use your back foot to come in, and your front foot to get out. Move in and out quickly with each combination…moving in with each strike, and then moving out and circling after the combination is complete.Imagine push kick, jab, cross.Excellent technique. Eyes open.Jab, jab, front hook, back hook.Keep breathing…blocking…moving…get in fast…get out fast.Jab, cross, hoon kick.In and out quickly…circling…good fighting stance.Front cut kick, back cut kick, cross, uppercut.Speed and power…focused and confident.Hook, uppercut, uppercut.Breathing with each strike. Getting quickly in and out.Clinch, knee, knee, knee, break.Moving quickly. Agile. Focused. Powerful.Hook, cross, horizontal elbow.Breathe.Now imagine other combinations that you can use to attack your opponent. Imagine responding to each opening you see…striking when your opponent is open.See yourself anticipating each movement your opponent makes. Before your opponent can land a strike, you have already moved…already blocked…already used a strike of your own.Imagine using combinations.(Pause)Let’s relax for a few moments as you allow the martial arts training techniques and skills to become permanently stored in your memory.Just relax. Focus your mind completely on these words. Breathing calmly…doubling your relaxation with each breath.Sinking deeper…floating…relaxing…drifting…Feeling so pleasant…so comfortable…completely at ease…confident…(Pause)Now think about areas of martial arts training that have been challenging for you. Perhaps a particular technique has been difficult in the past. Maybe there is a habit you have been wanting to change. Identify a martial arts training area that has been difficult.Now see yourself with a new, positive habit. See yourself perfectly completing the technique.(Pause)You use this technique perfectly every time. With precision. Speed. Power. Control. This former challenge has become your new strength.Thinking about this former challenge causes you to be filled with a feeling of confidence.Assuming a fighting stance and doing kickboxing or Muay Thai moves causes you to perform each movement with precision…causes you to breathe effectively…to keep your eyes open during the movement…to utilize effective blocks.Entering a ring in a martial arts competition causes you to be energized, confident, and strong.Your kicks, punches, knees, elbows, movements, breathing, and blocks come naturally, automatically, and easily.You are in tune with your opponent, responding before your opponent even completes a movement.You are fully confident and highly skilled.Training causes you to feel energetic. Each exercise you do causes you to feel wonderful. Every moment you spend training causes you to feel great.(Pause)Doing this relaxation exercise causes you to feel confident and calm and to pursue your scheduled training with confidence and motivation. Getting quickly in and out.Clinch, knee, knee, knee, break.Moving quickly. Agile. Focused. Powerful.Hook, cross, horizontal elbow.Breathe.Now imagine other combinations that you can use to attack your opponent. Imagine responding to each opening you see…striking when your opponent is open.See yourself anticipating each movement your opponent makes. Before your opponent can land a strike, you have already moved…already blocked…already used a strike of your own.Imagine using combinations.(Pause)Let’s relax for a few moments as you allow the martial arts training techniques and skills to become permanently stored in your memory.Just relax. Focus your mind completely on these words. Breathing calmly…doubling your relaxation with each breath.Sinking deeper…floating…relaxing…drifting…Feeling so pleasant…so comfortable…completely at ease…confident…(Pause)Now think about areas of martial arts training that have been challenging for you. Perhaps a particular technique has been difficult in the past. Maybe there is a habit you have been wanting to change. Identify a martial arts training area that has been difficult.Now see yourself with a new, positive habit. See yourself perfectly completing the technique.(Pause)You use this technique perfectly every time. With precision. Speed. Power. Control. This former challenge has become your new strength.Thinking about this former challenge causes you to be filled with a feeling of confidence.Assuming a fighting stance and doing kickboxing or Muay Thai moves causes you to perform each movement with precision…causes you to breathe effectively…to keep your eyes open during the movement…to utilize effective blocks.Entering a ring in a martial arts competition causes you to be energized, confident, and strong.Your kicks, punches, knees, elbows, movements, breathing, and blocks come naturally, automatically, and easily.You are in tune with your opponent, responding before your opponent even completes a movement.You are fully confident and highly skilled.Training causes you to feel energetic. Each exercise you do causes you to feel wonderful. Every moment you spend training causes you to feel great.(Pause)Doing this relaxation exercise causes you to feel confident and calm and to pursue your scheduled training with confidence and motivation. Each time you do this relaxation exercise you do relax twice as quickly and deeply as the previous time. Listening to this relaxation exercise causes you to feel wonderful.Now you have completed this martial arts training guided imagery. It is time to reawaken your mind and body so you can resume your regular activities.In a moment I will count to three. When you hear the number three you you are completely awake and alert, filled with energy and confidence.One…Two…Three.Continue from Martial Arts Training Guided Imagery toPreparation for Martial Arts CompetitionBack toRelaxation ScriptsBack toInner Health Studio Home

Before your opponent can land a strike, you have already moved…already blocked…already used a strike of your own.Imagine using combinations.(Pause)Let’s relax for a few moments as you allow the martial arts training techniques and skills to become permanently stored in your memory.Just relax. Focus your mind completely on these words. Breathing calmly…doubling your relaxation with each breath.Sinking deeper…floating…relaxing…drifting…Feeling so pleasant…so comfortable…completely at ease…confident…(Pause)Now think about areas of martial arts training that have been challenging for you. Perhaps a particular technique has been difficult in the past. Maybe there is a habit you have been wanting to change. Identify a martial arts training area that has been difficult.Now see yourself with a new, positive habit. See yourself perfectly completing the technique.(Pause)You use this technique perfectly every time. With precision. Speed. Power. Control. This former challenge has become your new strength.Thinking about this former challenge causes you to be filled with a feeling of confidence.Assuming a fighting stance and doing kickboxing or Muay Thai moves causes you to perform each movement with precision…causes you to breathe effectively…to keep your eyes open during the movement…to utilize effective blocks.Entering a ring in a martial arts competition causes you to be energized, confident, and strong.Your kicks, punches, knees, elbows, movements, breathing, and blocks come naturally, automatically, and easily.You are in tune with your opponent, responding before your opponent even completes a movement.You are fully confident and highly skilled.Training causes you to feel energetic. Each exercise you do causes you to feel wonderful. Every moment you spend training causes you to feel great.(Pause)Doing this relaxation exercise causes you to feel confident and calm and to pursue your scheduled training with confidence and motivation. Each time you do this relaxation exercise you do relax twice as quickly and deeply as the previous time. Listening to this relaxation exercise causes you to feel wonderful.Now you have completed this martial arts training guided imagery. It is time to reawaken your mind and body so you can resume your regular activities.In a moment I will count to three. When you hear the number three you you are completely awake and alert, filled with energy and confidence.One…Two…Three.Continue from Martial Arts Training Guided Imagery toPreparation for Martial Arts CompetitionBack toRelaxation ScriptsBack toInner Health Studio Home
